disease_associations.disease: Non-small cell lung cancer / lung adenocarcinoma
Head and neck squamous cell carcinoma

druggability_notes: EGFR is a well-established therapeutic target with a cytoplasmic ATP-binding tyrosine kinase domain (nucleotide-binding, autophosphorylation residues) and ligand-binding ectodomain. Clinical drug classes include: (1) EGFR tyrosine kinase inhibitors (e.g., afatinib) for EGFR-mutant NSCLC; (2) bispecific antibodies (e.g., amivantamab) plus chemotherapy for uncommon mutations such as G719A; (3) anti-receptor monoclonal antibodies for overexpressed EGFR (R-HSA-5638303). Oncogenic variants include EGFRvIII and ligand-responsive cancer variants (R-HSA-1236382, R-HSA-5637810). Molecular docking against PDB ID 1XKK indicates a druggable small-molecule binding pocket (e.g., phytochemical aspidinol binding affinity −8.6 kcal/mol).

functions: Tumor suppressor inducing growth arrest or apoptosis depending on physiological context and cell type
Sequence-specific DNA-binding transcription factor that activates or represses target genes upon binding to response elements
Regulation of apoptosis via stimulation of BAX and FAS expression and repression of BCL2
Cell cycle checkpoint control and arrest via transactivation of CDKN1A/p21 and prevention of CDK7 kinase activity in CAK complex
DNA damage response and promotion of DNA repair
Maintenance of genomic stability
Activates oxidative stress-induced necrosis in cooperation with mitochondrial PPIF (largely transcription-independent)
Regulates the circadian clock by repressing CLOCK-BMAL1-mediated transactivation of PER2

disease_associations.disease: Head and neck squamous cell carcinoma

druggability_notes: Contains a zinc ion-binding DNA-binding domain with available 3D structures. MDM2/MDM4 interaction interface is a validated therapeutic target; MDM2-p53 antagonist brigimadlin (vs doxorubicin, NCT05218499/Brightline-1) showed 22.3% ORR in MDM2-amplified dedifferentiated liposarcoma, though PFS primary endpoint was not met. Pro-apoptotic activity is modulated by ASPP-family regulators: PPP1R13B/ASPP1 and TP53BP2/ASPP2 activate, while PPP1R13L/iASPP inhibits — representing additional protein-protein interaction interfaces for therapeutic modulation. Tetramerization domain loss is a recognized cancer mechanism (R-HSA-9723905), making this domain also relevant. p53 IHC staining patterns serve as surrogate for TP53 mutation status. Germline pathogenic TP53 variants are NCCN-recognized prostate cancer risk variants.
cellular_states: Cells in G1 and G2/M cell cycle arrest

functions: Forkhead box (FOX) transcription factor that shapes chromatin accessibility and defines senescence-specific enhancer landscapes
Stimulates H3K27 acetylation and chromatin opening at de novo enhancers of pro-inflammatory SASP genes
Mesenchymal transcription factor driving epithelial-mesenchymal transition (EMT) transcriptional signatures
Promotes myeloid differentiation while suppressing T-cell differentiation in CD34+ hematopoietic progenitors
Transcriptional activator of EZH2, mediating epigenetic silencing of DKK3 via H3K27me3
Upregulates antioxidant proteins FDX1 and HO-1, reducing labile iron pool and ROS
Activates viral chromatin by acetylation to promote alphaherpesvirus (HSV-1) lytic replication and reactivation from latency
Lung endothelial-specific transcription factor binding the S1PR1 promoter; regulates lung vasculature and development

disease_associations.disease: T-cell acute lymphoblastic leukemia / mixed phenotype acute leukemia
Pulmonary fibrosis — context-dependent: tumor_suppressor in fibrotic mesenchyme; role may vary by cell type

cellular_states: Lung endothelial cells / brain microvascular endothelial cells (BBB)
Developing embryonic tissues (heart, digestive tract, pancreas, trachea, ureter)
druggability_notes: FOXF1-mediated enhancer remodeling proposed as a target for modulating senescent cells to promote healthy aging. FOXF1/FENDRR B-ALL subtype may benefit from early immunotherapy (blinatumomab) or targeted interventions. Identified as a potential therapeutic target in pulmonary fibrosis (iron/ROS-FOXF1 feedback loop) and renal fibrosis (FOXF1-EZH2-DKK3 axis). Acts via epigenetic cofactors p300/CBP (EP300/CREBBP) at viral and host chromatin. As a nuclear DNA-binding transcription factor, direct small-molecule binding is challenging; no specific binding pockets or drug classes described in database sources.

functions: Homologous recombination-mediated DNA double-strand break repair

E3 ubiquitin-protein ligase activity (Lys-6-linked polyubiquitin chain formation via BRCA1-BARD1 heterodimer)
Transcriptional regulation (acts as transcriptional activator; coordinates with RNA Pol II)
Maintenance of genome integrity
Regulation of centrosomal microtubule nucleation
Required for FANCD2 targeting to sites of DNA damage
Inhibits lipid synthesis by binding inactive phosphorylated ACACA and preventing its dephosphorylation

disease_associations.disease: Breast cancer (including hereditary breast and ovarian cancer syndrome, HBOC)

cellular_states: Early embryonic cells during maternal-to-zygotic transition (MZT) and germ layer formation
Cells undergoing DNA damage response after ionizing radiation
Cells in S-phase and G2/M cell cycle checkpoints
ccRCC cells (BRCA1/2-WT, where BRCA1 is degraded via SLFN11)
druggability_notes: BRCA1/2-mutated tumors are sensitive to PARP inhibitors (e.g., olaparib, niraparib, fluzoparib) via synthetic lethality with HRR deficiency. BRCA1/2-WT ccRCC is resistant to PARPi monotherapy but can be sensitized via SLFN11-dependent vulnerability with alkylating agents (TMZ, streptozocin). SLFN11 drives proteasomal degradation of BRCA1 protein, inversely correlating with BRCA1 expression. PARP1-PARP2 trapping at replicative gaps is a determinant of PARPi sensitivity. BRCA1 contains a RING-domain (zinc-finger) conferring E3 ubiquitin-ligase activity; functions through the BRCA1-BARD1 heterodimer interface and PALB2/BRCA2-RAD51 machinery — both relevant to synthetic-lethal therapeutic contexts. Novel BRCA1 variants in prostate cancer cluster in DNA repair domains including the coiled-coil and RAD51-binding regions.

functions: GTPase functioning as a molecular switch in RAS signal transduction
Binds GDP/GTP and possesses intrinsic GTPase activity
Activates downstream MAPK/ERK signaling (activates MAPK1/MAPK3 leading to phosphorylation and degradation of GJA1)
Modulates PI3K-AKT signaling
Drives oncogenic proliferation when mutated
Promotes tumorigenesis and immunosuppressive tumor microenvironment when mutated
Promotes transcriptional silencing of tumor suppressor genes in colorectal cancer via ZNF304
Substrate for LZTR1-mediated ubiquitination by a BCR (BTB-CUL3-RBX1) E3 ligase complex
pathways: RAS signaling downstream of NF1 loss-of-function variants

disease_associations.disease: Invasive mucinous adenocarcinoma of the lung

druggability_notes: Direct KRAS inhibitors (KRASi) including KRAS G12D-specific inhibitor MRTX1133; allosteric SHP2 inhibitors targeting upstream KRAS signaling; combination strategies with ERK inhibitor (Ulixertinib), PI3K inhibitor (BKM-120), and PERK inhibitor to overcome resistance. Acquired resistance to KRAS inhibitors via ATF3-ASNS asparagine metabolic reprogramming is a major therapeutic barrier. KRAS Q61, G12D and other mutational subtypes (Q61H, Q61L) have distinct clinical/treatment relevance. Protein undergoes prenylation and palmitoylation for membrane targeting; no explicit druggable pocket beyond the mutant-selective switch-II pocket described for covalent inhibitors.
